name: NavCard
description: Outlined, rounded single-row navigation card. Single label (optional supporting line) + auto-rendered trailing chevron over a `surface`-toned box with a hairline `outlineVariant` inset-shadow stroke and `radius.md` corners. Whole card is the click target — keyboard focus, hover overlay, and tap commit all sit on the card. Reach for it when one drill-in row needs to read as its own discrete affordance rather than as one entry in a [List/nav](../list/nav.md) stack.

label.description: Primary card text. Single line; truncates with ellipsis.

supportingText.description: Secondary line under the label. Single line; truncates with ellipsis.

leading.description: Optional leading slot — a 16px icon glyph (renders in `currentColor`) or a 32-rung [Thumbnail](../thumbnail/thumbnail.md). When omitted the label sits flush at the inline padding edge.

trailingIcon.description: Overrides the auto-rendered chevron. Pass a custom 16px glyph when the drill target isn't another navigation surface (inline expand-down arrow, external-link arrow).

appearance.description: Container fill. `default` paints `sys.color.surface`. `transparent` removes the fill so the host surface (e.g. a `surfaceContainerLow` tonal band, a Section body) reads through — outline, label, and chevron stay intact.

href.description: When provided, renders as `<a href>` instead of `<button>`. Mutually exclusive with `onClick` as the primary commit, though either may be combined for analytics.

forcedState.description: Docs-only — pins the card to a single visual state via `data-force-state`. Not for production use.

container.description: Outlined rounded box. `surface` fill, `radius.md` corners, hairline `outlineVariant` stroke painted as inset box-shadow (never `border:`). Whole container is the interactive target.

leading.description: Optional leading slot at 16 × 16 (icon) or 32 × 32 (Thumbnail). Icon glyphs render in `currentColor`. Vertically centred against the label column.

labelCol.description: Vertical column holding label and (optional) supportingText. `min-width: 0` so both lines truncate.

label.description: Primary card text. 16px / Regular / `onSurface`. Single line; truncates with ellipsis.

supportingText.description: Secondary line under the label. 14px / Regular / `onSurfaceVariant`, 2px below the label. Single line; truncates.

trailingIcon.description: Auto-rendered right-pointing chevron at 16px, `onSurfaceVariant`. A `trailingIcon` prop overrides.

default.note: Opaque surface fill. Use when NavCard sits on a transparent or distinct-tone host (page background, white space between sections).

transparent.note: No fill — the host surface tone reads through. Reach for it when the card sits on a tonal band (a `surfaceContainerLow` strip, a Section's body, a BottomSheet's content slot) and should read as 'attached to' that tone rather than as its own opaque tier. Outline, label, chevron, and hover overlay are unchanged.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual painted as a three-layer ring on the card's outer edge. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the card is in.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: NavCard sits as its own bounded surface with margin to siblings; an outward ring reads cleanly without colliding with a neighbouring row's stroke.

ring.implementation: outset box-shadow on the container's `::after` overlay; the rest stroke stays painted on `::before` so the two layers don't fight.

focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

behavior.clickTarget: Whole card is clickable. The trailing chevron is never a separate hit target.
behavior.truncationNotWrap: Both label and supportingText truncate; the card never grows to fit long text.
behavior.elementSwap: Renders `<button>` by default; `<a href>` when `href` is set.

forbidden: border: painted as the rest stroke — must be inset box-shadow (no-layout strokes rule, DESIGN.md)
rest outline removed — the outlined chrome IS the defining shape of NavCard; without it the component is a list/nav row in disguise
stacked vertically with raw <div> gaps — group via the NavCardGroup wrapper so the 8px gap stays consistent across compositions
tightly stacked into a hairline-divider rail — for that anatomy use list/nav (NavCards in a NavCardGroup must each remain a discrete outlined card)
text-decoration underline on label or any descendant on hover — the whole card is the hit target with its own hover overlay; an underline doubles the affordance
trailing chevron replaced with a non-navigational glyph when the action is not a drill-in — use button/icon or a different component instead
destructive commit fired on card tap — destructive commits open a Dialog / BottomSheet, never fire inline
